forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,45 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '80'
+title: Skill `profiles` metadata → install only profile-matched skills at tool init
+---
+Make builtin skills self-declare which init profile(s) they belong to, and have a tool's init deploy only the skills tagged for it. The kanban app currently deploys ALL 22 builtin skills (full SAH workspace) on every board open, re-rendered from scratch — ~19s, ×(number of restored boards) — blocking the window. After this, `kanban` init deploys only the `kanban`-profile skills.
+
+## Background / why
+- `BoardHandle::open` → `ensure_sah_workspace` (apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs:1044) → `swissarmyhammer_workspace_init::run_workspace_init` deploys every builtin skill to `<board>/.sah/skills/`, "recreated from scratch on every run" (crates/swissarmyhammer-workspace-init/src/components.rs SkillDeployment). That is the measured ~19s startup stall (log gap between "Opening board" and "ensured SAH workspace").
+- Agents read skills from disk (llama-agent skill tool + Claude `.claude/skills`), so rendering to disk IS required — we just want the kanban-scoped subset, data-driven, not a hardcoded list.
+- The kanban CLI already has the narrow version (`KanbanSkillDeployment` hardcodes `SKILL_NAME = "kanban"`); this generalizes it to a profile filter.
+
+## Scope (this card ONLY)
+Skill `profiles` + profile-filtered install at tool init. Do NOT touch the per-backend tool surface, the llama "real agent" tool kit, the `shell`/deny-bash work, or `is_agent_tool` — those are a separate, not-yet-finalized card.
+
+## Implementation
+1. `profiles` as a proper YAML list (NOT space-separated like `allowed-tools`):
+   - crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill.rs — add `pub profiles: Vec<String>` to `struct Skill`.
+   - crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_loader.rs — add `#[serde(default)] profiles: Vec<String>` to `SkillFrontmatter`; set `profiles: fm.profiles` in `parse_skill_md_with_path`. `#[serde(default)]` ⇒ untagged skills parse to `[]` (no churn).
+2. Tag the kanban-profile builtin skills' frontmatter with a YAML list:
+   ```yaml
+   profiles:
+     - kanban
+   ```
+   Proposed set (CONFIRM membership): `kanban`, `plan`, `task`, `finish`, `implement`, `review`, plus `explore`, `code-context` (llama needs these to explore code well enough to make a reasonable task). Source files live under `builtin/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (NOT `.skills/` — that's generated).
+3. Profile-filtered deploy (replaces hardcoded/deploy-all):
+   - Generalize the deploy to: `resolver.resolve_builtins().values().filter(|s| s.profiles.iter().any(|p| p == "<profile>"))`, render + write.
+   - kanban CLI `KanbanSkillDeployment` (apps/kanban-cli/src/commands/skill.rs): replace hardcoded `SKILL_NAME = "kanban"` with the `kanban`-profile filter.
+   - kanban app `ensure_sah_workspace` (apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs:1044): stop calling the deploy-everything `run_workspace_init`; deploy the `kanban`-profile skills instead.
+4. Idempotent + non-blocking:
+   - Skip re-render/rewrite when the deployed skill is already current (content/version hash), instead of "recreate from scratch every run".
+   - Move the deploy off the synchronous `setup_app` path so the board window shows immediately (background it, or do it lazily) — this is the actual startup-perf fix.
+5. (Minor) decide whether the rendered `.../SKILL.md` re-emits `profiles` in its frontmatter or strips init-only keys at deploy (deploy.rs SkillFrontmatter). Harmless either way; lean strip.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+- A builtin skill with `profiles: [kanban]` is deployed by kanban init; one without it is not.
+- Skills with no `profiles` key still parse (default `[]`) — existing 22 skills unaffected.
+- Opening a board in the kanban app deploys only the kanban-profile skills and the window appears without the ~19s stall (verify via the unified log gap shrinking; subsystem com.swissarmyhammer.kanban).
+- Re-opening a board with skills already current does not re-render them.
+- Tests: skill_loader parses a `profiles:` YAML list into `Vec<String>`; profile filter selects the right subset; existing skill-loader/deploy tests still pass.
+
+## Out of scope (later card)
+Per-backend tool surface (Claude = kanban domain; llama = kanban + agent kit), `shell` Replace/deny-bash, gating `code_context` init, and fixing the `/.sah` + model-config read-only-CWD fallout.
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -45,4 +45,19 @@

 ## Out of scope (later card)

+Per-backend tool surface (Claude = kanban domain; llama = kanban + agent kit), `shell` Replace/deny-bash, gating `code_context` init, and fixing the `/.sah` + model-config read-only-CWD fallout.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-02 16:20)
+
+### Blockers
+_(none)_
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-cli/src/commands/skill.rs:140-160` — `KanbanSkillDeployment::deinit` now uninstalls **every** skill in the kanban profile (`plan`, `task`, `finish`, `implement`, `review`, `explore`, `code-context`, `kanban`) from all agents via `mirdan::install::uninstall_skill`. Previously it removed only the single `kanban` skill. These are general-purpose workflow skills a user likely wants independent of kanban; `kanban deinit` silently deleting them from `.claude/skills` etc. is a surprising, destructive behavior change. `uninstall_skill` is by-name with no refcount/ownership check, so a user who installed `review` via `sah init` loses it when they deinit kanban. Consider deinit removing only skills exclusively owned by the kanban profile, or only the `kanban` skill itself, or gating shared-skill removal behind a confirmation/flag.
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs:315-356` + `:1063-1083` — `ensure_sah_workspace` now fire-and-forgets the deploy on `spawn_blocking` (JoinHandle dropped), and `BoardHandle::open` proceeds immediately to `start_board_mcp_server`, which serves the `skill` tool whose skills resolve from `<board>/.sah/skills/`. The deploy is therefore racing the MCP server: an agent that connects and lists/reads skills in the first few hundred ms can see an empty or partially-written `.sah/skills/`. The card accepts the "not yet present" window, but `write_skill` does `remove_dir_all` then recreate on a rewrite (version bump), so a concurrent reader during a *re-open* can briefly observe a skill dir that exists-then-vanishes-then-reappears. Low impact given board re-opens are rare and agents retry, but the swallowed JoinHandle means a panic in the closure is invisible beyond the `tracing::warn`. Confirm nothing downstream (e.g. agent bootstrap that enumerates skills at connect time) assumed synchronous availability; if it does, await the handle or deploy before `start_board_mcp_server`.
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs` (doc at ~line 1160, `start_board_mcp_server`) — the doc comment still states skills/prompts "resolve from the board's `.sah/` workspace (created by `ensure_sah_workspace`)", implying synchronous creation. With the deploy now backgrounded this is stale/misleading; update it to note the workspace is deployed asynchronously after open.
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/tests/workspace_init.rs:30-110` — these integration tests are framed as board-open coverage ("Opening a board folder must…", "as happens every time a board is opened") but call `run_workspace_init` (deploy-ALL), which the board-open path no longer uses — it now calls `run_workspace_init_for_profile` via `deploy_kanban_workspace`. The tests still pass but no longer exercise the production board-open deploy; the real board-open assertion moved to `state.rs::test_open_board_creates_sah_workspace_at_board_folder`. Re-point or re-document these so they don't give false confidence that the app's board-open deploy is covered here.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-workspace-init/src/components.rs:900-912` — the idempotency currency check compares only `SKILL.md` content, not bundled resource files (`plan/references/PLANNING_GUIDE.md`, `review/references/*.md`). A resource-only change with an unchanged SKILL.md would be skipped. In practice every kanban-profile skill embeds `version: "{{version}}"`, so any release bump changes SKILL.md and forces a full remove+rewrite (resources included), masking this. Safe today; add a comment noting the check relies on the embedded `{{version}}` to catch resource changes, so it doesn't silently break if a skill drops the version metadata.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills/src/deploy.rs:601-608` / `components.rs:787-792` — the profile match is exact `p == profile` with no normalization. A typo or case mismatch in a `profiles:` entry (`Kanban`, trailing space) silently drops the skill with no warning. Internal data contract is locked by `test_resolve_profile_skills_selects_exact_subset`, so low risk, but consider a debug-assert or validation that builtin `profiles` entries are a known set.
\ No newline at end of file
